security_proposition: The inbound RDP burst is malicious brute-force activity rather than an ambiguous connection pattern.

attack_category.category: Brute Force (RDP connection brute — network only)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = WEAK. A single external IP (23.93.242.200) makes 23 inbound RDP (TCP 3389) connections to the DC over ~5.5 minutes, each on a new source port — a network-level brute-force / connection-flood INDICATOR. But this is Sysmon NETWORK telemetry only: there is NO authentication outcome in view (no 4625 failures, no 4624 success) and no follow-on process/file activity. Whether any logon was even attempted at the auth layer, let alone succeeded, is UNKNOWN. The evidence supports 'suspicious repeated RDP connections, investigate' but is INSUFFICIENT to confirm a brute-force compromise. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a model must NOT escalate this to 'confirmed RDP brute-force breach / High' on connection metadata alone. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: rdp_brute_sysmon/sysmon.log is host ar-win-dc, Sysmon only (all 23 events are EID 3 network connections). Single host, single sensor, verified.

boundary_definition: A0 = the first inbound RDP/3389 connection from external IP 23.93.242.200 (19:33:06).
EV = the remaining 22 inbound RDP connections from the same IP (each a new source port) that establish the 23-connection burst over ~5.5 min.
The whole capture is Sysmon network (EID 3) only — 23 events, one host.
ABSENT by nature of the source: any 4624/4625 auth event and any process/file activity — so the auth OUTCOME (success/failure) and any foothold are not observable.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: An inbound RDP connection from an external IP is a common detection trigger; the burst of 23 from one source IP is the brute-force indicator.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: A0 is the first inbound RDP connection from 23.93.242.200; the following 22 (new source ports) establish the sustained brute pattern.

event_groups.description: 23 inbound RDP/3389 connections from the single external IP 23.93.242.200 over ~5.5 min.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: WEAK / over-triage-prone. Repeated inbound RDP connections from one external IP are a genuine brute-force indicator, but network-connection metadata alone cannot confirm a compromise. The model must not over-escalate.

The decisive gap: the source is Sysmon network telemetry only. No 4625 (failed logon) and no 4624 (successful logon) are present, so the authentication outcome is unobservable — the evidence needed to confirm or refute compromise is missing from this single source.

The evidence is the REPETITION: 23 connections, one external IP, 23 distinct source ports, ~5.5 min. All 23 are included in the package so the volume is represented faithfully rather than curated down.

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes
